title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
Two macrophage cell states expressing CD68 are present in healthy skin. Mac1 shows
higher expression of complement transcripts (C1QB, C1QC) and scavenger receptors
(CD163, MARCO)

macrophages are the
dominant MP in first trimester skin
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify Mac1 (Macro_1) as one of two CD68+ macrophage states in healthy adult skin, characterised by high complement transcripts (C1QB, C1QC) and scavenger receptors (CD163, MARCO). Macrophages are also the dominant mononuclear phagocyte in first trimester fetal skin.

title: Inflammatory Skin Disorders: Monocyte-Derived Cells Take Center Stage
quotes: The altered Mac1/Mac2 ratio identified by scRNA-seq in skin lesions in Reynolds et al. might corroborate with decreased CD14bright CD64bright CD163+ MNP_3 and increased CD14dim CD64bright CD163+ MNP_4 Mf clusters detected by FCM in psoriatic L skin
summary: Mehta et al. (2021) discuss the Mac1/Mac2 ratio in psoriatic skin, noting that Mac1 (CD14, MARCO, CD163) and Mac2 (F13A1, CD163) are altered in disease, with complement-expressing Mac1 decreased in psoriasis lesions.

title: Macrophages in Skin Wounds: Functions and Therapeutic Potential
quotes: macrophage receptor with collagenous structure (MARCO) + macrophages were consistently identified in two studies and express high levels of the complement proteins' (C1QB and C1QC) transcripts and the cell surface receptor CD163
summary: Sim et al. (2022) confirm that MARCO+CD163+C1QB+C1QC+ macrophages (corresponding to Mac1/Macro_1) are consistently identified across human skin macrophage studies.
